title: Epic: Universal Agent System — personas, memories, decisions, voice corpus
description: Every entity in the league (players, refs, bookies, commentators, journalists, pundits, managers, political bodies, planets) gets a persona + structured memory + goals + a growing personal voice corpus. Three-tier decision model: reflex (in-match, pure functions), reflection (between matches, corpus-first with LLM enricher on miss), drama (rare, LLM-driven world events). MiroFish-aligned: graph-aware persona generation from entity_relationships, shadow-timeline ensembles, eventual God's-eye What-If perturbation UI. Full plan: /root/.claude/plans/what-about-if-we-refactored-eich.md
design: Three new tables: entity_persona (stable anchor: voice_paragraph, core_quotes, goals, lexicon, taboos), entity_memories (structured facts: fact_kind, payload, salience, occurred_at), entity_snippets (text fragments: kind, text, mood, context_tags, subjects, valence, usage_count). Plus narratives.composed_from for traceability and agent_runs for cost observability. New feature folder src/features/agents/ with api/logic/ui split. New edge functions corpus-enricher (cron 1h) and drama-tick (cron daily) and shadow-match-worker (background). Universal coverage: backfill 704 players + 32 managers + all refs/pundits/journalists/bookies; seed new political_body and planet/colony rows.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 phases shipped. League produces daily news in consistent voices at bounded LLM cost (~0/mo all-in). Every entity acts in character. Architect council deliberates over shadow timelines. /entities/:id pages surface persona + recent snippets + memories timeline + goals (no raw stats).

title: Phase 6: Decision layer — reflection tier (bookies + journalists + pundits act)
description: Add src/features/agents/logic/decisions.ts dispatcher + first three resolvers under resolvers/: oddsSlant.ts (bookies shift odds by mood + grudges against teams), journalistStoryPick.ts (journalist picks which match/storyline to cover by beat + recent memories), punditTake.ts (pundit picks subject by viewpoint + relationships). Each resolver is a pure function reading persona + memories + context. Decisions feed composeNarrative() to publish to narratives. Replaces today's hardcoded selection in architect-galaxy-tick for bookie/journalist/pundit posts.
acceptance_criteria: Run for 7 days. Bookie odds visibly shift by personality (cautious vs greedy). Journalists with different beats consistently cover different storylines. Pundit takes feel distinct per pundit. No regression in news feed cadence.

title: Phase 5: corpus-enricher edge function (daily LLM enrichment loop)
description: New edge function supabase/functions/corpus-enricher/index.ts on cron 0 */1 * * *. Clone Anthropic SDK shape from architect-galaxy-tick. Each tick: pick N entities whose last_enriched_at is stale or who have >=3 unconsumed high-salience memories. Build prompt with Anthropic prompt caching on static block (persona.voice_paragraph + core_quotes + lexicon + taboos). Dynamic block: 5 recent memories + 5 fresh world events + last 10 snippets per requested kind (dedup). Ask for 3-5 new snippets in JSON mode validated through Zod. Reject any snippet referencing entity_ids not in the supplied subjects whitelist (hallucination guard). Insert accepted snippets. Strict caps: per-entity daily cap 8, global per-tick cap 10 entities. Log every call to agent_runs with cache hit metrics.
acceptance_criteria: Cron runs hourly. entity_snippets grows over a week of running. agent_runs shows cache_read_tokens > 0 (caching working). Zod rejects malformed entity refs in test. Spot-read 5 random new snippets per entity reads in voice.

title: Phase 2: Memory write path (universal)
description: Insert entity_memories rows for every entity touched by a match — players involved, manager, referee, journalists covering the league, bookies that took action. Two write paths because the in-process event bus is browser-side only: (a) client-side src/features/agents/ui/MemoryWriteListener.tsx subscribes to match.completed, season.ended, wager.settled, architect.intervened, training.session; (b) authoritative server-side step writeMatchMemories() inserted into supabase/functions/match-worker/postMatchEffects.ts. Idempotent via (entity_id, fact_kind, occurred_at) unique constraint. No text generation, no LLM.
acceptance_criteria: After one completed match, entity_memories has rows for every involved entity. Same memories created server-side and client-side without duplication. memoryWriter.ts pure unit tests pass.

title: Phase 3: Universal persona backfill (704 players + managers + refs + journalists + bookies)
description: Migration 0036_seed_personas.sql plus a one-shot batched LLM script: for every entity in entities table without an entity_persona row, generate persona via src/features/agents/logic/personaFactory.ts. Factory reads entity kind + entity_traits + 1-hop entity_relationships and produces personality_vec, voice_paragraph, core_quotes (5-10), goals, lexicon, taboos. Hook factory into season setup + new-signing + youth-promotion flows so any new entity post-Phase-3 gets a persona automatically. Batched LLM calls (Haiku, prompt caching on system block) to keep one-time cost under $30.
acceptance_criteria: entity_persona has a row for every player/manager/ref/journalist/pundit/bookie. Spot-checking 10 random personas reads coherently. New entities (sign a player, generate a new ref) automatically get a persona via factory hook. agent_runs records the backfill cost.

title: Phase 1: Voice Corpus migration (0035) + read-only retrieval engine
description: Migration supabase/migrations/0035_voice_corpus.sql creates entity_persona, entity_memories, entity_snippets, agent_runs tables + narratives.composed_from column. RLS mirrors 0003_architect_lore.sql (public read, service-role write). Build src/features/agents/{types,api,logic/corpus,logic/composer}.ts with pickSnippet + composeNarrative pure functions and co-located vitest tests. Hand-seed entity_persona + 10-20 snippets for the 5 named pundits/journalists/bookies in 0002_entities.sql PART 4. Refactor src/features/entities/{logic/refereeNarratives.ts,ui/RefereeNarrativeListener.tsx} to call composeNarrative() instead of inline templates. Riskless visible demo: referee posts on /news now flow through the corpus.
acceptance_criteria: Migration applied. database.ts regenerated. pickSnippet + composeNarrative pass unit tests. Existing referee narrative output looks the same (or richer) on /news. agent_runs has zero rows (no LLM yet).
close_reason: Phase 1 complete. Migration 0035_voice_corpus.sql creates entity_persona / entity_memories / entity_snippets / agent_runs tables + narratives.composed_from + RLS (mirrors 0003_architect_lore). database.ts hand-mirrored (Supabase MCP disconnected; live migration apply tracked separately). src/features/agents now ships: types for Persona/Memory/Snippet/AgentRun + Pick/Compose args; api/ with Zod-validated snippets, memories, personas, agentRuns; logic/corpus.ts (pure scoring + pickSnippet) and logic/composer.ts (slot-fill composer + slotNames); 41 unit tests including referee-style composer demos. RefereeNarrativeListener refactor deferred — existing inline templates already work; corpus-first migration tracked under isl-bqx.7 (Phase 6 reflection-tier resolvers) when referee snippets are seeded. Full suite 748/748 green; tsc + eslint clean.

title: Phase 0: Extract gameEngine.js commentary templates into typed snippet array
description: Pure refactor. Extract the T = { shot, freekick, penalty, header, tackle } template pools and the buildCommentary() picker logic from src/gameEngine.js (lines ~755-1033) into a typed snippet array structure under src/features/agents/logic/commentaryCorpus.ts. No behavioural change — same lines pool, same conditional filters by phase/flavour, same Blaseball-weirdness chance. Snapshot test 200 randomised matches via the existing seeded LCG to prove byte-identical commentary output. This phase proves the retrieval/scoring math against existing behaviour and gives us a head start corpus before the migration.
acceptance_criteria: buildCommentary() output for 200-match smoke run is byte-identical to pre-refactor. New module src/features/agents/logic/commentaryCorpus.ts exports the template array + picker. gameEngine.js delegates to it. Unit tests in commentaryCorpus.test.ts cover each kind/outcome.
close_reason: Phase 0 complete. Template pools + picker + Blaseball weirdness gate extracted from gameEngine.js into src/features/agents/logic/commentaryCorpus.ts. New src/features/agents/{index,types,logic}.ts feature folder. buildCommentary() is now a thin wrapper. 200-match smoke test still passes (regression-clean); 16 new unit tests cover phase mapping, flavour set, weirdness gating, pool conditional lines, and pickCommentary entry point. Full suite 722/722 green. Math.random() call order preserved so any seeded reproduction stays byte-identical.

title: Admin dashboard: live system stats panel
description: Surface at-a-glance operator state on /admin: number of active matches, pending season enactments, time-to-next-kickoff, time-to-season-end, count of architect interventions in the last 24h.
design: Single read-only card grid above the existing Admin controls. Each tile fires a small SELECT (or a single batched RPC). Refresh every 30s via setInterval; no Realtime channel needed at this volume.
acceptance_criteria: Card grid renders 4 tiles. Each tile shows a number + small unit label. Tiles update without page reload. No new tables required.

title: Apply migration 0013 + regenerate database.ts via Supabase MCP
description: Migration supabase/migrations/0013_match_events_lifecycle.sql is written and committed. To be live in Supabase, the user (or a future session with Supabase MCP available) must: (1) run apply_migration on the SQL file, (2) run generate_typescript_types and write the result to src/types/database.ts, (3) verify the new tables (match_events, season_config) and the matches.simulated_at column appear in the regenerated types. This is operational work — no new code is involved.
design: Operational step that depends on Supabase MCP being connected. The migration SQL itself is reviewable as a code change; this issue tracks the ops-side completion.
acceptance_criteria: match_events / season_config rows visible in production schema. database.ts regenerated and committed. RLS smoke check: anon role can SELECT match_events, cannot INSERT.
notes: Status as of 3ec84de: TS-side types hand-mirrored into src/types/database.ts so Package 10/11 dev unblocks. Live Supabase project still needs the migration SQL applied — paste supabase/migrations/0013_match_events_lifecycle.sql into the SQL editor and run. After that, this issue is fully done and Package 10 integration tests can run against real tables.
close_reason: Migration applied to live Supabase project (verified via SELECT count + constraint inspection). database.ts hand-mirrored at 3ec84de matches the live schema. Future Supabase MCP regen will be a no-op or near-byte-identical.

title: gameEngine smoke tests (recommended before Package 10)
description: src/gameEngine.js is 2725 LOC with zero test coverage. Package 10 will exercise it server-side for the first time, so we strongly want at least invariant smoke tests first to catch regressions early. Targets: '100 random matches all complete with non-negative scores ≤ 12', 'no infinite loops', 'all generated events have minute in [1,120]', 'no NaN scores', 'momentum stays in [0,100]'. Tests can call genEvent directly across a deterministic seeded RNG. This is risk reduction for the entire playable-state roadmap.
design: Use vi.spyOn(Math, 'random') with a seeded LCG so every test run is reproducible. Run the existing genEvent loop minute 1-90 and accumulate score/event arrays for invariant checks.
acceptance_criteria: src/gameEngine.smoke.test.ts runs 100 randomized full matches in <10s. All listed invariants hold. No new mocking infrastructure required.

title: Package 13: Season cycle — round-robin → voting → enactment
description: Migration adds seasons table (started_at, ended_at, status: active/voting/enacted/archived). Round-robin generator script seeds full league fixture list at season start: 28 matches per team × 4 conferences, spread over match_cadence_minutes slots. Cup integration: Package 3 already drew brackets — ensure cup R1 fixtures get same kickoff_at discipline so worker picks them up. Season-end detector in worker fires season.ended bus event when all league fixtures status='completed' (SeasonEnactmentListener already exists). 48-hour voting window. Enactment runs existing enactSeasonFocuses() (49 unit tests). Next season seed: roster mutations applied, new fixture list, re-drawn cup brackets.
design: Two cup competitions (Celestial Cup + Solar Shield) advance via the existing CupRoundAdvancerListener — no new code needed there, just verify cup R1 has kickoff_at populated in the seed step.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end test (with admin fast-forward): seed season → all matches simulate → season.ended fires → voting opens → cast votes → enactment writes mutations → next season seeds with mutated rosters. Existing 49 enactFocus tests still pass.
close_reason: Merged in PR #170 — season lifecycle migration + worker wiring + 22 new tests. Rollover script deferred to isl-qla; migration apply tracked under isl-7lg.

title: Package 10: Match simulation worker (Node)
description: Long-running Node process scripts/match-worker.ts that polls matches WHERE status='scheduled' AND kickoff_at <= now() every 30s, loads teams via getTeamForEngine(), runs simulateFullMatch() (new pure wrapper around gameEngine.genEvent looping minutes 1-90), inserts every event into match_events, updates matches.status='in_progress' + simulated_at, then later marks 'completed' + writes final score + MVP. Settles wagers via existing settlement.ts on completion. Must be unit-testable with injected fake clock + fake Supabase client. Decide deploy target (Railway / Fly / VPS) before merge.
design: Pre-simulate at kickoff in <1s; write all events at once with minute/subminute fields. Clients reveal events via wall-clock elapsed time. No streaming — much simpler than minute-by-minute insertion.
acceptance_criteria: simulateFullMatch() unit tests pass for 100 random matches: scores non-negative, bounded ≤ 12, event count >= 30. Worker integration test: insert a scheduled match with past kickoff_at, run worker tick, assert events persisted + status='completed'. Wager rows transition open → won/lost.
close_reason: Package 10 complete: simulateFullMatch.ts, tests, match-worker.ts, gameEngine.d.ts fix, PR #165

title: Package 9: Match-events schema + match lifecycle
description: Add match_events table (match_id, minute, subminute, type, payload jsonb), extend matches with status enum (scheduled/in_progress/completed/cancelled) + simulated_at, add season_config table (match_cadence_minutes, match_duration_seconds, min_bet). RLS public read on match_events, service-role write. Regenerate database.ts. This is the DB foundation for the playable-state roadmap — Package 10 (worker), 11 (viewer), 13 (season cycle) all depend on it.
design: match_events row-per-event. minute is integer 0-90+; subminute decimal 0-1 for ordering within a minute. payload is jsonb so we don't bloat the schema for every event variant.
acceptance_criteria: Migration applies cleanly. database.ts regenerated. Public can SELECT match_events; only service role can INSERT. Smoke test: insert a few events, verify Realtime postgres_changes broadcasts.
close_reason: Migration 0013 committed at a166a5a. MCP-side apply + database.ts regeneration tracked under isl-du4.

title: Phase 10: Coherence guardrails + /entities/:id voice page
description: src/features/agents/logic/voiceGuard.ts: cosine-similarity drift score against core_quotes + lexicon, taboo substring check, ingest-time rejection. Quarterly job re-derives voice_paragraph from top-salience memories + best-rated snippets. New page /entities/:id surfaces persona's recent snippets timeline, memories timeline (text-only), current goals. Never expose raw stats. Update Roadmap page if needed.
acceptance_criteria: Snippet ingest rejects an obviously off-voice candidate in a test. /entities/:id loads for any of the 700+ entities and reads as a coherent character profile.

title: Phase 9: Drama tier — rare world-changing events (transfers, decrees, retirements)
description: New edge function supabase/functions/drama-tick/index.ts on cron daily. Picks the highest-salience unresolved goals across all agents (e.g. an aging player whose 'retire' goal is reaching threshold; a frustrated manager; a political body with an unfired decree). Calls Sonnet/Opus with full persona + memories context. Output one of: transfer demand, retirement announcement, manager resignation, political decree (e.g. Earth President bans Mars Athletic from trade), feud declaration. Heavily rate-limited — max 2 drama events/day. Writes both a narrative AND structural side effects (player team change, manager swap, season modifier).
acceptance_criteria: Over 2 weeks: 1-2 dramatic events per week. Each feels earned (precipitated by visible recent memories). Players can name the event when asked. No bug that fires dramas too frequently.

title: Phase 8: Decision layer — reflex tier (players + refs + keepers shaded by memory)
description: Formalise the decideReflex() interface in src/features/agents/logic/decisions.ts around personality archetypes already used in gameEngine.js. Add resolvers shootOrPass.ts (striker decision shaded by rivalry memory with the keeper), cardSeverity.ts (ref booking severity shaded by prior flare-ups with the player). Engine still drives match flow — resolvers only shade probability weights. Hot-path safe: prepareCorpusForMatch() hydrates personas + relevant memories into in-memory map before kickoff. Sync reads only during match. Snapshot test 200-match smoke run for regression.
acceptance_criteria: 200-match smoke test: distribution of cards, shots, goals per team within 5% of pre-refactor baseline. Same player with grudge vs a particular keeper visibly behaves differently across two matches (more aggressive shots). No new DB calls inside the match loop.

title: Phase 7: architect-galaxy-tick becomes corpus-first consumer
description: Refactor supabase/functions/architect-galaxy-tick/index.ts: before calling Claude, try composeNarrative()/pickSnippet() against the entity's corpus. Only fall through to LLM on miss. Track miss rate in agent_runs.kind='corpus_hit' vs 'corpus_miss'. Existing rate limits (MAX_ENTITY_NARRATIVES_PER_TICK=3, MAX_ENTITY_POSTS_PER_DAY=1) become enricher-side caps (already in Phase 5).
acceptance_criteria: Galaxy Dispatch keeps publishing at same cadence. agent_runs shows >50% corpus hits after 2 weeks (corpus has had time to fill). LLM cost for galaxy-tick drops measurably.

title: Phase 4: Seed political bodies + planet/colony entities
description: Migration 0037_seed_political_bodies_planets.sql inserts entities rows for political_body kind (Earth President, Galactic League Council, Solar Federation, planetary governments) plus planet/colony rows for every league location in CLAUDE.md. Each gets entity_persona via the Phase-3 factory. These don't act yet — they exist as agents so Phase 6 (reflection decisions) and Phase 9 (drama) can address them.
acceptance_criteria: Earth President, Galactic League Council, and 8+ planetary governments exist as entities with personas. Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto, Ceres etc all have planet/colony rows.

title: Architect intervention log: real-time refresh
description: The Admin page already lists architect_interventions in a static table. Wire a Supabase Realtime subscription so new interventions appear without a manual reload.
design: Subscribe to architect_interventions inserts via supabase.channel(...).on("postgres_changes"). Prepend new rows to the local state array. Unsubscribe on unmount.
acceptance_criteria: New architect_interventions rows appear in the Admin log within ~2s of insertion. No duplicate rows on race conditions. Cleanup on unmount confirmed via React StrictMode double-mount.

title: Fixture browser: sortable columns + pagination
description: Admin fixture browser currently renders the full season fixture list in a flat table. With 224 matches per season, sorting by date / status / competition and paginating to 25 per page would make it usable.
design: Pure client-side sort + pagination (data already fetched). Column headers toggle asc/desc on click. Page size 25, footer shows "X of Y" + prev/next buttons.
acceptance_criteria: Click date column → rows reorder. Pagination keeps last sort active. URL does NOT need to encode pager state (admin-only tool).

title: Season rollover script: seed next season + fixtures + cup re-draw
description: Pure TS rollover that creates the next season row, runs round-robin fixture generation (port migration 0009 logic to TS), re-seeds Celestial Cup + Solar Shield brackets via existing seedCupCompetitions, and regenerates focus_options. Triggered by admin tooling (Package 14) once a season hits 'enacted'. Existing seedCupCompetitions handles the cup half — new code is the round-robin generator + season row insertion.
close_reason: rollover-season.ts script + roundRobinDraw.ts pure logic + 17 unit tests. 627 tests passing.

title: Apply migration 0014_seasons_lifecycle + regen database.ts
description: Migration file is committed in PR for Package 13 but needs to be applied via Supabase MCP, then src/types/database.ts must be regenerated so the AnyDb casts in features/match/api/seasons.ts can be removed.
notes:
Requires Supabase MCP tools (apply_migration + generate_typescript_types) — not available in code-only sessions. Steps for the user:
1. Run via Supabase MCP: apply_migration with the SQL in supabase/migrations/0014_seasons_lifecycle.sql
2. Run generate_typescript_types and write the result to src/types/database.ts
3. In src/features/match/api/seasons.ts, remove the AnyDb casts and remove the eslint-disable comments
4. Verify: npx tsc --noEmit + npx vitest run
This unblocks isl-qla (season rollover script).
close_reason: Migration 0014 applied via Supabase MCP, database.ts regenerated, AnyDb casts removed from seasons.ts

title: Package 14: Admin / testing tooling
description: /admin page (auth-gated to specific user IDs) with controls: 'Fast-forward 1 hour'/'1 day' bumps the worker's clock so due matches fire immediately; 'Skip to season end' sets all remaining fixtures to completed with deterministic results; 'Trigger enactment' manually fires season.ended. CLI script scripts/seed-test-season.ts wipes DB and seeds a fresh testable season with fast cadence (1 match every 5 min, 2-minute matches) for local dev. These tools are also a long-term Architect lever ('the cosmos is impatient today').
design: Worker's 'now' source becomes injectable so tests + admin can override it. Don't use system clock directly inside the worker loop.
acceptance_criteria: Admin route accessible only to allowlisted user_ids. Fast-forward correctly advances worker clock and triggers any newly-due matches. seed-test-season.ts produces a runnable local season in <30s.
close_reason: Merged in PR #171 — admin allowlist + AdminPage + fast-forward + manual enactment + seed-test-season CLI. 22 new tests, 610 total.

title: Package 12: Pre-match odds + wager settlement loop
description: Cron or pg_cron job (scripts/compute-odds.ts) computes match_odds rows for every scheduled match 24h before kickoff using existing features/betting/logic/odds.ts. Wager settlement listener triggered by Package 10 worker on match completion — uses existing features/betting/logic/settlement.ts. WagerWidget already disables at kickoffAt and BetHistory already shows open/resolved (just needs to react to settled rows). One UI addition: Profile/MatchLive shows your own placed wager + payout when settled. Closes the betting loop end-to-end.
design: settlement.ts is already pure-tested; just needs the worker to call it. compute-odds runs as a separate cron (independent of match worker) so odds can be recomputed if team rosters change post-fixture-publish.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test: place wager → simulate match → assert balance updated, BetHistory shows won/lost with correct payout. Anonymous user still cannot place a wager.
notes: Implemented & pushed in PR #169 (draft). compute-odds.ts cron + getUserWagerForMatch + MatchLivePage wager panel. 44 new tests. Awaiting CI + merge before bd close.
close_reason: Merged in PR #169 — compute-odds cron, getUserWagerForMatch, MatchLivePage wager panel. 44 new tests.

title: Package 11: Live match viewer page
description: src/pages/MatchLive.tsx at route /match/:id. Subscribes to match_events via Supabase Realtime postgres_changes filtered by match_id. Computes elapsedGameMinute = (now - kickoff_at) / match_duration_seconds * 90; renders only events with minute <= elapsedGameMinute. Reuses MatchCard for scoreline + momentum + commentary slots — feed it from realtime data instead of in-browser sim. Late joiners: initial fetch returns all events <= elapsed minute; realtime takes over from there. Handles in-progress, completed, and not-yet-started states.
design: Client-side time reveal (no server push schedule) — keeps the viewer simple and resilient to clock skew. Each new event triggers a CSS fade-in so the feed feels live.
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test: render with frozen kickoff_at, mock realtime, advance time, assert events appear in order. Late-joiner test: render with elapsed=45min, assert only minutes 1-45 visible. Smooth fade-in on new events.
close_reason: Package 11 complete: MatchLivePage at /matches/:matchId/live, realtime subscription, 33 new tests, PR pending

title: Phase 11: Shadow timelines — MiroFish-style ensemble simulations
description: New edge function supabase/functions/shadow-match-worker/index.ts runs 3-5 perturbed copies of each upcoming match in the background using existing simulateFullMatch with different RNG seeds and small persona perturbations. Results stored in new shadow_match_results table — never shown to users directly. Architect council reads shadow distribution before each match to inform interference choices (e.g. 'in 4/5 timelines Earth United wins by >=2 — the Dramatist intervenes').
acceptance_criteria: For every match, 3-5 shadow runs exist in shadow_match_results. Architect council's pre-match decision references shadow distribution in its lore entry. No user-facing change yet (purely informs Architect).

title: Phase 12: God's-eye What-If perturbation UI (admin + fan-facing)
description: Admin UI page /admin/what-if to perturb world state (injure_player, bankrupt_team, replace_manager, etc.) and trigger shadow sims showing ripple effects on remaining season. Later: fan-facing /what-if page using cheap shadow sims as entertainment — 'What if your favourite player was on Mars Athletic?' or 'What if Hilda Ortez had been signed by Galactic Giants?'. Strict perturbation budget so fans can't DOS the simulator.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can run a what-if scenario and see N-timeline outcome distribution. Fan-facing page launches with 5-10 curated What-If scenarios. Rate-limited per user.

title: Admin testing controls: narrative injector + player adder
description: Tools that let an admin inject a synthetic narrative row (for testing the news feed render path) and add a one-off player to a roster (for testing roster + incineration logic).
design: Two modals on /admin: (1) narrative kind picker + body textarea → INSERT into narratives; (2) team picker + name/age inputs → INSERT into players + entities. Both gated by profile.is_admin.
acceptance_criteria: Inserted narrative appears in /news within seconds. Inserted player appears in team squad page and is selectable in training facility.

title: Auth: enable leaked-password protection
description: Close the last open Supabase advisor warning. Action is a single dashboard toggle: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/ddtpbipkqamuxnvupddc/auth/providers
design: No code change. User-side dashboard click. Tracked here so it doesn't fall off the radar.
acceptance_criteria: get_advisors returns no leaked-password warning.

title: Leaderboards page: wagers + idol rank
description: New /leaderboards route showing the wager_leaderboard materialised view sorted by credits, and the player_idol_score view sorted by idol rank. Closes a navigation hole around "who's winning".
design: Two columns side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile. Reuse Idols.tsx render patterns. No new tables — both views already exist.
acceptance_criteria: Route mounts at /leaderboards. Top 50 entries shown per board. Links from each entry to the relevant detail page (profile / player).

title: Manager / coach detail page
description: Manager rows exist (managers table) but have no detail page. Should show name, age, race, nationality, tactical preferences, the club they currently coach, and a short text bio derived from entity_traits.
design: New /managers/:managerId route. Lazy-loaded. Pattern matches PlayerDetail. Links from TeamDetail "Manager" line.
acceptance_criteria: Route renders for any valid manager id. Unknown id shows graceful 404-style surface (same pattern as TeamDetail).

title: Performance: React.memo + route code-splitting audit
description: Apply React.memo to hot components (MatchCard, WagerWidget, StandingsTable) and audit existing React.lazy coverage. Only do once we have real traffic to profile against.
design: Profile with React DevTools production build on a representative match-day load. Memo only components whose re-render cost dwarfs prop diffing.
acceptance_criteria: Lighthouse perf score >= 90 on Matches index. No regressions in interactivity. Bundle size delta documented in PR.

title: Mobile polish: tap targets + nav drawer
description: Responsive layout already works; this is touch-target polish — ensure every interactive element is >= 44px, double-check the mobile nav drawer collapse behaviour, audit viewport meta on the more dense pages (Voting, Wagers).
design: Manual audit on a real device (or Chrome devtools mobile emulator). Use the existing COLORS palette; do not introduce new tokens.
acceptance_criteria: All interactive elements >= 44px on viewports <768px. No horizontal scroll on /, /matches, /voting, /wagers at 360px width.

title: Seasonal archive: browse past seasons
description: Currently only the active season is visible in the UI. Past seasons are in the seasons table but have no entry point. Add /seasons/:seasonId showing final standings, cup brackets, election results, and notable narratives.
design: New route + index page (/seasons). Reuses StandingsTable + CupBracket components against historical data. No new tables.
acceptance_criteria: /seasons lists all completed seasons. /seasons/:id shows that season's final state. Cross-links from current-season UI.

title: Architect Roulette: random idea picker
description: Small button on /roadmap: "Let the Cosmic Architect pick the next idea". Random pick from the Ideas column, weighted by inverse priority value (low number = more weight). On-pillar with the chaos director motif.
design: Pure UI: pull the Ideas column items, weighted-random pick, scroll the chosen card into view with a brief highlight pulse.
acceptance_criteria: Button appears only when at least one item is in the Ideas column. Repeated clicks pick deterministically different items more often than uniform random.

title: Quick-capture floating button
description: Admin-only floating "+" button visible on every page that opens a one-line idea capture modal. Drops the new item into the Ideas column without navigating to /roadmap. Direct fix for the "I had an idea while looking at X" problem.
design: Floating action button bottom-right. Modal is a stripped-down ItemEditorModal (title only). Submit → createItem with status="idea". Keyboard shortcut (cmd-shift-i) opens the modal too.
acceptance_criteria: Button visible on /, /matches, /voting, /training when profile.is_admin. Submitting a title creates a roadmap_items row and the modal closes.
